mission.title: helm: app-name has no deployed releases
mission.description: app-name has no deployed releases. Requested by 94+ users.

steps.title: Check current helm deployment
steps.description:
Verify your helm version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
helm list -n helm 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working helm installation.

steps.title: Review helm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
kubectl get configmap -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=helm
```
**Output of `helm version`:**
```
$ helm version 
Client: &version.Version{SemVer:"v2.13.1", GitCommit:"618447cbf203d147601b4b9bd7f8c37a5d39fbb4", GitTreeState:"clean"}
Server: &version.Version{SemVer:"v2.13.1",

steps.title: Apply the fix for app-name has no deployed releases
Upgrade helm to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade helm:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade helm helm/helm --namespace helm
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm
helm list -n helm
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
kubectl get events -n helm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "app-name has no deployed releases" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The code in place was originally correct. Removing `status: deployed` from the query results with Helm finding the latest release to upgrade from, regardless of the state it is currently in which could lead to unintended results. It circumvents the problem temporarily, but it introduces much bigger issues further down the road.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
